untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires identifying two V-shaped bottoms at similar price levels with a prior downtrend, which requires multi-bar pattern recognition logic not expressible through simple indicator comparisons. The 'confirmation line' (highest high between two bottoms) and the requirement to identify 'pointed, V-shaped (not rounded) turns' both require structural pattern analysis beyond the allowed indicator operators. While Donchian upper band was used as a proxy for breakout confirmation, true AADB pattern identification requires custom pattern detection unavailable in this rule engine.
translation_notes: Attempted to translate using Donchian Channel upper band as a proxy for the confirmation line breakout. Entry condition uses crosses_above for snappy close above confirmation. Exit uses a reasonable reversal (close below 20-day SMA). Stop loss set to 5% based on failure rate data. However, the core pattern mechanics (identifying two bottoms, V-shape validation, prior downtrend confirmation) cannot be fully captured without pattern recognition logic.
